name: Daejeon (South Korea)

colors.symbolism: forms of the emblem and the wordmark
the rivers and sky of Daejeon in the emblem

description: A teal field charged with a stylised multicolour emblem — a yellow flower/sun with blue and white forms — and the city name in Korean beneath.
history: Daejeon, a Metropolitan City (Gwangyeoksi) since 1989, adopted its current flag and city emblem in 1997. The mark combines stylised abstractions of the city's name (대전 / 大田, 'Great Field'), the surrounding mountains, the rivers that converge at Daejeon and a rising sun, expressing the city's identity as the heart of Korea's research and high-technology sector.

features.symbolism: abstract motif blending stylised forms of the character 大 ('great'), the rivers that converge in the city, the surrounding mountains and a rising sun
